text.title: AGENTS.md for Receiving Team Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every AGENTS.md for Receiving Team application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: The file MUST contain 4 sections: project context, agent rituals (commands the team runs, hooks they expect), gotchas + landmines, no-go zones for the AI agent. Missing sections are rejected.
rule.rationale: The four sections cover the four failure modes new teams hit; dropping any one re-creates the failure mode within weeks.

rule.statement: Sections MUST be written in the voice and conventions of the receiving team's agent (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.), not the leaving team's. Generic phrasing is rejected.
rule.rationale: AI-agent context windows are tight; framing matters; a Claude-tuned file reads differently from a Cursor-tuned one, and mismatched framing reduces adherence.

rule.statement: The no-go-zone section MUST name file globs OR concrete operations (e.g. 'never let the agent touch /migrations/ without dual approval'). Generic warnings are rejected.
rule.rationale: AI agents do not act on advisory prose; they act on rules with observable triggers; concreteness is the only way the rule survives.

rule.statement: Receiving team MUST execute one real task using this AGENTS.md within 7 days of handover; any gap found is patched as v1.1 before signoff.
rule.rationale: Hand-over files that are never used decay invisibly; the validation gate forces the file to face reality before the leaver loses context.

rule.statement: The file MUST carry a version + last_reviewed; the receiving team commits to reviewing it quarterly. Files without a lifecycle clause are rejected.
rule.rationale: Stale AGENTS.md is worse than missing AGENTS.md because the agent acts on outdated rules; the lifecycle clause is the renewal anchor.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
